title: Evangelion guitar Tabs
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion guitar Tabs             Quote Post by scarmullet » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:15 pm I am Looking to find some guitar tabs for some Music in EVA. I already know A Cruel Angels Thesis, but I have searched for a long time for a Komm Susser Tod tab and Misato's theme, but I cant find a midi or a tab of the song (If I have a midi, I can run it through guitar pro and get a tab from that). If anyone has found or made one, show me.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:42 pm There's a bunch of piano sheet music that Evamonkey had on his site a while ago, but the link no longer resolves. Someone a while ago asked me to make tabs of some of the songs, but I can only find Asuka Strikes . If you can find the sheet music, I can make tabs.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:50 pm http://sharebee.com/2faf6edb Thats the sheet music I use on Piano when I have a chance to play.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 06, 2009 6:06 pm wait, if you can already read music, why do you need tabs?

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Mon Jul 06, 2009 6:07 pm Well, I rely on tabs for guitar, I cant seem to grasp what fret plays what note.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 06, 2009 7:15 pm Ornette wrote: There's a bunch of piano sheet music that Evamonkey had on his site a while ago, but the link no longer resolves.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 06, 2009 11:03 pm Blamo: http://www.tesuji.org/music/Komm_Susser_Tod.tab No idea how accurate those are, don't have a guitar with me so I did those blind. There's probably a few places that need reworking because it'll be a bit hard to play.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Tue Jul 07, 2009 1:16 am Thanks man, I'll try learning it.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Tue Jul 07, 2009 10:43 am I've gotta split for work shortly, but I can look into transcribing some songs if you'd like. I've got nothing better to do and it sounds fun.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Tue Jul 07, 2009 4:06 pm Anything from EVA

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 07, 2009 5:21 pm I found another: Eva-00 and Barefoot in the Park http://www.tesuji.org/Eva00.txt http://www.tesuji.org/Barefoot_in_the_park.txt I vaguely remember doing that for PronsAmuro over at ANF because he wanted to play those songs on his bass.

[]:             Quote Post by snowballreborn » Tue Jul 07, 2009 5:32 pm links are much appreciated :)

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Tue Jul 07, 2009 7:36 pm Sick! Sheet music! I'll transpose it for teh clarinet! w00t!!!

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Tue Jul 07, 2009 10:41 pm I'm doing well with the Komm Susser Tod tab, except for the part that goes "In my heart of hearts I know I could never love again I've lost everything Everything Everything that matters to me, Matters in the world." Other than that, its good.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu May 16, 2013 8:03 pm Ornette wrote: Blamo: http://www.tesuji.org/music/Komm_Susser_Tod.tab No idea how accurate those are, don't have a guitar with me so I did those blind. There's probably a few places that need reworking because it'll be a bit hard to play.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu May 16, 2013 10:23 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: Has anyone got any idea where I can find this now? Link is dead now unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu May 16, 2013 10:35 pm Ornette wrote: http://www.tesuji.org/music_related/Komm_Susser_Tod.tab

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Fri May 17, 2013 11:00 pm Now I wanna do a cover of Komm Susser Todd for rockquest this year. Daaaamn.

[]:             Quote Post by fizz450_03 » Thu Jun 27, 2013 7:07 pm oooh music to play with, time to get back to my rusted-as-f*ck piano skills... anyone here play these in conventions etc?

[]:             Quote Post by Theron » Thu Jun 27, 2013 11:50 pm I will annihilate for some Sin from Genesis and The Beast II/Normal Blood tabs.
